title: virola rugulosa
intro: Virola rugulosa is a species of plant in the family Myristicaceae, commonly known as the "shaggy canella" or "ayahuasca" plant. The medicinal properties of Virola rugulosa include its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, as well as its use in traditional medicine for treating various ailments. The health benefits associated with the plant include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory issues, among others. Virola rugulosa is traditionally prepared as a herbal remedy through infusion, where the bark of the plant is steeped in hot water, and sometimes used in combination with other plants for enhanced medicinal effects.
